examples.title: Basic wait() Usage
examples.description: Demonstrates basic wait() to prevent zombie process
examples.explanation: This program forks a child process. The child does some work and exits with status 42. The parent calls wait() which blocks until the child terminates, then retrieves the exit status using WIFEXITED and WEXITSTATUS macros.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is time child runs

examples.title: waitpid() with WNOHANG for Non-blocking Wait
examples.description: Shows how to use waitpid() with WNOHANG to avoid blocking
examples.explanation: The parent uses WNOHANG to check if child has terminated without blocking. This allows the parent to do other work while periodically checking the child's status. When waitpid() returns a positive value, the child has terminated.

examples.title: wait4() with Resource Usage
examples.description: Demonstrates wait4() to get resource usage information about child
examples.explanation: wait4() is used to wait for a specific child process (by pid) and retrieve resource usage information including user CPU time, system CPU time, and maximum memory usage (ru_maxrss).

examples.title: Handling Multiple Children with waitpid()
examples.description: Parent creates multiple children and waits for all to complete
examples.explanation: This demonstrates creating multiple child processes and waiting for all of them to complete using waitpid() with pid=-1 and WNOHANG option. The parent can do other work while periodically checking for terminated children.

examples.title: SIGCHLD Handler to Prevent Zombies
examples.description: Using signal handler to automatically collect child status
examples.explanation: A SIGCHLD handler automatically reaps zombie children when they terminate. Using WNOHANG inside the handler ensures we don't block. This prevents zombies without explicitly waiting in the main code.
